_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

ssh_attacks.failed_password_attempts: 50 in current auth.log segment (200 lines sampled)
ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 1 currently banned, 12990 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: fail2ban total bans 12990 (up from 12821, +169 since 12:28 UTC). 1 IP currently banned: 113.163.156.190. Continued brute-force from bot networks. No successful unauthorized logins — only sudo commands from novakj.

pdf_security.document_write_safe: 2 usages: keyboard-shortcuts.js line 416 (print cheat sheet) and presenter-view.js line 1216 (presenter popup). Both write to newly opened blank windows with static or internally-generated HTML only. Acceptable pattern.
pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 357+ usages across 137 JS files. All reviewed — no unescaped user/PDF-derived content in dangerous contexts, with tracked exceptions in known_issues.

selection_toolbar_js.task: NEW FILE — TASK-142: Contextual text selection toolbar — floating quick-action bar on text select
selection_toolbar_js.status: NEW FILE — reviewed in full (487 lines)
selection_toolbar_js.storage_usage: localStorage — boolean enable flag and hex color string only
selection_toolbar_js.innerHTML_safety: Single innerHTML at line 136: btn.innerHTML = '' — literal empty string to clear color-dot button content. SAFE.
selection_toolbar_js.text_display: All button labels via textContent (line 138). No user/PDF-derived content in DOM insertion.
selection_toolbar_js.url_construction: doSearchWeb() line 461: window.open('https://www.google.com/search?q=' + encodeURIComponent(text), '_blank', 'noopener'). Properly encoded, hardcoded safe domain, noopener set. SAFE.
selection_toolbar_js.event_bus_integration: Emits to annotate:applyToSelection, sticky-notes:addAt, redact:addFromSelection — all receiving modules previously reviewed and clean.
selection_toolbar_js.positive_notes: Good: no eval, no Function, no document.write, textContent for all labels, encodeURIComponent for URL params, noopener on window.open, proper DOM APIs throughout.

sticky_notes_js.task: MODIFIED — added bus.on('sticky-notes:addAt') handler for selection toolbar integration
sticky_notes_js.changes: Added event listener (approx lines 66-82) that creates a note from x/y/pageNum/text parameters. Follows existing note creation pattern — structuredClone, bounds clamping, no innerHTML.
sticky_notes_js.security_status: CLEAN — no security issues in changed code.

redact_js.task: MODIFIED — added bus.on('redact:addFromSelection') handler for selection toolbar integration
redact_js.changes: Added event listener (approx lines 36-52) that reads selection rects and creates geometry-only marks (x/y/width/height). No text content stored, no innerHTML.
redact_js.security_status: CLEAN — no security issues in changed code.

annotate_js.task: MODIFIED — added bus.on('annotate:applyToSelection') handler for selection toolbar integration
annotate_js.changes: Added event listener (approx lines 44-70) that creates annotation from selection rects with type/color from event. Uses existing getSelectionRects/renderAnnotation functions. selection.removeAllRanges() called after.
annotate_js.security_status: CLEAN — no security issues in changed code.

new_dependencies.note_external_sdks: cloud-storage.js loads 4 external SDKs dynamically (Google, Dropbox, MSAL). Not self-hosted. Currently blocked by CSP.

known_issues: MEDIUM: recent-files.js lines 407, 414, 620, 623, 630, 641: doc.fileName (from file.name -> IndexedDB) inserted into innerHTML without escaping — in aria-label, title, data-rf-name attributes and element content. A file with HTML special chars in its name (e.g., '">.pdf') would inject HTML. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline handlers/scripts. HTML structure manipulation still possible. Fix: import or define escapeHtml() and apply to all doc.fileName insertions in renderWelcomeRecent(), renderPanelContent(), and renderPanelCard().

MEDIUM: custom-stamps.js lines 823, 825: stamp.name in alt attribute and stamp.overlayText in element content inserted via innerHTML without escaping. Data from localStorage or imported JSON. A crafted import file with stamp.name='" onload="alert(1)' or stamp.overlayText='' would inject HTML. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline handlers/scripts. HTML structure manipulation still possible. Fix: add escapeHtml() to module and apply to stamp.name, stamp.overlayText.

MEDIUM: custom-stamps.js lines 916, 918: custom.name, custom.text (8 chars), custom.imageData, custom.textColor, custom.borderColor from localStorage/import inserted via innerHTML without escaping. Same import vector as above. Colors could break out of style attribute with double quotes. CSP mitigates. Fix: escape all user-controlled values or use DOM APIs.

MEDIUM: custom-stamps.js importLibrary() (line 1163-1199): imported JSON stamps only validated for structural fields (id, name, type). No sanitization of text content, imageData URL, overlay text, or color values. imageData not validated as data: URL — could be an external tracking URL. CSP blocks inline handlers. Fix: validate imageData starts with 'data:', sanitize text fields.

MEDIUM: macro-recorder.js line 520/804: imported data.color used directly in style='background:${macro.color}' without validation. A crafted import JSON with double-quote in color value could break out of style attribute. CSP blocks inline handlers. Fix: validate color matches /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}$/.

CRITICAL-FUNCTIONAL: cloud-storage.js loads 4 external SDK scripts (apis.google.com, accounts.google.com/gsi, dropbox.com, alcdn.msauth.net) but CSP script-src 'self' blocks them all. The cloud storage feature is entirely non-functional. To fix: either (a) self-host the SDKs in /lib/ and add SRI hashes, or (b) add the 4 domains to CSP script-src. Option (a) is strongly preferred for security — external SDKs are supply chain risk without SRI. Option (b) broadens attack surface.

MEDIUM: cloud-storage.js line 525: r.fileName and r.path from localStorage (originating from cloud provider API responses) inserted into innerHTML without escaping — in title attribute and element content. Cloud file names can be set by other users sharing files. A shared file named '.pdf' injects HTML. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline scripts/event handlers. HTML structure and CSS injection still possible. Fix: add escapeHtml() and apply to r.fileName and r.path in renderRecentFiles() template.

MEDIUM: cloud-storage.js line 663: f.name and f.id from Microsoft Graph API inserted into innerHTML without escaping — in data-name, data-id attributes and element content. OneDrive filenames can be set by file sharers. Double-quote in f.id could break data-id attribute. Fix: escape f.name and f.id in showOnedrivePicker() template.

MEDIUM: form-detect.js line 709: field.label from PDF text content inserted into innerHTML without escaping — both in title attribute and element content. A malicious PDF with crafted text items could inject HTML. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline scripts/event handlers. HTML structure manipulation and CSS injection still possible. Fix: add escapeHtml() and use on field.label in renderResultList() template.

MEDIUM: font-inspector.js line 997: f.subsetPrefix (PDF font subset prefix) inserted into innerHTML without escaping. Validated by /^[A-Z]{6}$/ regex at extraction but data originates from PDF. CSP blocks script execution. Fix: apply escHtml(f.subsetPrefix).

MEDIUM-LOW: macro-recorder.js lines 202, 206: escHtml() used in input value attributes but does not escape double quotes. Macro name/description containing double quotes can break out of value attribute. Data from user prompt or imported JSON. CSP blocks inline script execution. Self-XSS/import vector. Fix: use escAttr() for attribute contexts.

MEDIUM-LOW: macro-recorder.js importMacro() (lines 768-815): validates _type and steps existence but does not validate name type/length, description type/length, color format, step.condition structure, or step.params content. Steps with unrecognized actionIds are stored and rendered but cannot execute. Low exploitability — import is user-initiated.

MEDIUM-LOW: preflight.js line 617: r.summary inserted into innerHTML without escaping. Most summaries are internally-generated numbers/strings. Color space summary (line 216) interpolates PDF-derived color space names (csName from pdf.js operator args). Error fallback (line 574) interpolates err.message. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline scripts. HTML structure manipulation theoretically possible. Fix: apply escapeHtml() to r.summary in renderResults().

MEDIUM-LOW: form-tab-order.js line 224: field.type from PDF form field data (via getFormFields()) interpolated unescaped in <small> element content via innerHTML. A crafted PDF could set field type to HTML. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline handlers. MEDIUM-LOW risk. Fix: escape field.type or use textContent.

MEDIUM-LOW: form-tab-order.js importOrder(): imported groups array not schema-validated. group.color used via DOM API (style.borderLeftColor) — CSS-only injection, no XSS vector. entry.groupId assigned without validation. Low exploitability — user-initiated import.

MEDIUM-LOW: form-validation.js line 1022: cond.operator and cond.ruleType from fvState.conditions interpolated unescaped in innerHTML element content. Conditions can be imported via importRules() without schema validation. A crafted import JSON with operator='' injects HTML. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline handlers. Fix: apply escHtml() to cond.operator and cond.ruleType.

MEDIUM-LOW: form-validation.js line 1055: f.type (PDF form field type from getFormFields()) interpolated unescaped in option element text via innerHTML. Same pattern as form-tab-order.js. CSP mitigates. Fix: escape f.type.

MEDIUM-LOW: form-validation.js line 1060: r.type used in option value without escAttr(). describeRule() output includes unescaped rule.params.presetName (line 353). Both can come from imported JSON. Could break out of option value attribute. CSP mitigates. Fix: use escAttr() for option value, escHtml() for option text.

MEDIUM-LOW: form-validation.js importRules() (lines 1299-1333): imported JSON only validates data.rules existence. No schema validation on rules structure, conditions, operator values, ruleType values, or regex patterns. Same lax import pattern as ~10 other modules. User-initiated import only.

MEDIUM-LOW: voice-commands.js line 561: renderAliases() inserts alias phrase (user-set via prompt or custom input) and action name into innerHTML in both element content and data-phrase attribute. Self-XSS only — user sets own aliases in localStorage. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline handlers. Same pattern as ~15 other modules. Fix: add escapeHtml() and apply to phrase; use DOM setAttribute for data-phrase.

MEDIUM-LOW: voice-annotations.js importMemos() (lines 973-1001): imported JSON audioData not validated as data: URL. A crafted import could include external URLs, but base64ToBlob() catches parse errors producing empty blob — not exploitable. Same pattern as custom-stamps.js imageData. Fix: validate audioData starts with 'data:'.

MEDIUM-LOW: version-manager.js line 395: v.name in title attribute not double-quote escaped — escHtml() uses DOM textContent->innerHTML which does not escape double quotes. v.name from imported ZIP manifest could break out of title attribute. v.id in data-id attributes (lines 393, 405-408) safe when self-generated but unvalidated on import. CSP mitigates script execution. Fix: add escAttr() for attribute contexts or validate imported entry fields.

MEDIUM-LOW: version-manager.js importVersions() (lines 296-333): imported manifest entries not schema-validated. No checks on entry.id format, entry.name length, or entry structure. Imported entry.id used directly in data-id HTML attributes. Low exploitability — import is user-initiated from user's own files, CSP blocks scripts.

MEDIUM-LOW: auto-link.js escapeHtml() (line 389) doesn't escape double quotes. Used in data-key, data-al-key, data-al-dismiss attributes (lines 348-354) where r.key contains PDF text content. CSP script-src 'self' blocks inline handlers. Same pattern as ~15 other modules. Fix: escape double quotes or use DOM APIs for attribute setting.

MEDIUM-LOW: page-tabs.js line 930: tab.label (user input from prompt() or imported JSON file) inserted into innerHTML without escaping. Also in aria-label attribute at line 928 without quote escaping. navigatorSearch at line 901 in value attribute without quote escaping. No escapeHtml function defined. CSP blocks script execution. Self-XSS only — user sets own labels or imports own JSON. Fix: add escapeHtml() and apply to label/search values.
LOW: auto-link.js CSV export (lines 562-563) d.text quoted but not protected against CSV formula injection. Self-data only. Same pattern as cross-search.js.
LOW: auto-link.js no processing timeout on full-document scan. Iterates all pages. Client-side self-DoS only. Same pattern as other analysis modules.

LOW: form-validation.js line 648: new RegExp(rule.params.pattern) from user/imported regex pattern — ReDoS possible with pathological patterns. try/catch handles syntax errors but not catastrophic backtracking. Self-DoS only.

LOW: form-validation.js lines 812, 835, 863: querySelector with PDF-derived fieldId in attribute selector without CSS.escape(). Could cause SyntaxError with crafted field IDs. Same pattern as annotation-replies.js. Not exploitable for XSS.

LOW: toolbar-manager.js line 572: custom preset name (user text input) and key (from localStorage) inserted into innerHTML in data-preset attribute and element content without escaping. Self-XSS only — user sets own preset names. CSP blocks script execution.
LOW: font-inspector.js line 1056: page number p used in data-page attribute without escAttr(). Pages are integers from PDF parsing. Minimal real-world risk. Fix: apply escAttr(p).

LOW-FUNCTIONAL: repair.js line 376: import path './lib/pdf-lib.min.js' should be '../lib/pdf-lib.min.js' since file is in /js/. Fallback to window.PDFLib exists so module still works. Not a security issue.

LOW: find-replace.js line 301: new RegExp(query, flags) from user search input — ReDoS possible with pathological patterns but self-DoS only. try/catch handles invalid regex. Same pattern as link-manager.js and cross-search.js.

LOW: cross-search.js regex mode: new RegExp(query, flags) from user search input — ReDoS possible but self-DoS only. try/catch, MAX_RESULTS_PER_DOC limit, zero-length match guard, and AbortController all mitigate.
LOW: cross-search.js CSV export: snippets/filenames could contain =,+,-,@ prefixes interpreted as formulas by spreadsheet apps. Self-data only (user's own PDFs/filenames).

LOW: annotation-replies.js findAnnotationElement() lines 610-617: annotKey from imported data used directly in querySelector attribute selectors without CSS.escape(). Crafted keys could cause SyntaxError or match unintended elements. Not exploitable for XSS — only returns element reference. Self-XSS/import vector only.
LOW: contact-sheet.js, booklet.js, nup.js: no explicit processing timeout — renders/embeds all pages serially. Client-side self-DoS for very large PDFs at high scale. LOW risk.
LOW: multi-window.js: no explicit limit on number of pop-out windows. popoutCounter increments unboundedly. Client-side self-DoS only — user-initiated. LOW risk.

LOW: scan-enhance.js: no explicit processing timeout on multi-page enhancement loop. OUTPUT_SCALE=2.0 means ~9MB ImageData per page. Cancel button provides user escape. Client-side self-DoS only. Same pattern as svg-export.js, contact-sheet.js, booklet.js, nup.js.

LOW: presenter-view.js sendThumbnails() renders all pages at 128x96 scale with no page count limit or timeout. For 1000-page PDF could generate significant rendering load and BroadcastChannel traffic. Client-side self-DoS only. Same pattern as contact-sheet.js, booklet.js, nup.js.

LOW: preflight.js no processing timeout — all 10 checks iterate all pages. Ink coverage renders every page at 0.15 scale. Client-side self-DoS only for very large PDFs. Same pattern as contact-sheet.js, booklet.js, nup.js.
LOW: macro-recorder.js no overall macro execution timeout. Steps execute sequentially with 500ms delay. User can abort via fail dialog. Same pattern as batch.js.
LOW: macro-recorder.js no limit on imported macro step count. Could cause slow rendering with very large step lists. User-initiated import only.

LOW: slide-transitions.js importPreset() (lines 623-651): no schema validation on imported JSON. Accepts any values for global/easing/duration/etc. Not exploitable — values used only in numeric contexts, switch case matching with fallthrough, and easing key lookup with fallback. User-initiated import only.

LOW: doc-summary.js: TextRank builds O(n^2) sentence similarity matrix with no explicit timeout. Client-side self-DoS only for very large documents. isProcessing flag prevents concurrent runs. Same pattern as other analysis modules.

LOW: camera-scanner.js: no explicit processing timeout on edge detection / perspective correction pipeline. Bounded by video frame dimensions (max 1920x1080 from getUserMedia constraints) and 6000x6000 output cap. Client-side self-DoS only.
LOW: object-editor.js: activate() scans all pages without processing timeout. Client-side self-DoS only for very large PDFs. Same pattern as contact-sheet.js, booklet.js, nup.js.
LOW: object-editor.js: blob URLs from thumbnailUrl not revoked on deactivate/new PDF load. Memory leak, not security issue.
server_tokens set to 'build' - leaks nginx version (nginx/1.26.3 Ubuntu) in Server header. Should be 'off'.
No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. Any new .md file added to webroot would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed but no firewall enforces this.
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot.
CGI location block exists in nginx but no CGI scripts present - dead config that could be removed.
Codex proxy at /codex returns 502 - backend not running. No security risk but dead endpoint.

offline.html line 84: inline onclick='window.location.reload()' handler is blocked by CSP script-src 'self'. Try Again button non-functional. Functional bug, not security issue. Fix: add inline <script> with addEventListener or use 'unsafe-hashes' CSP directive for the specific hash.

merge.js, batch.js, hyperlinks.js, bates.js, command-palette.js, keyboard-shortcuts.js, form-data.js, link-manager.js, reading-progress.js, bookmark-editor.js, layers.js use escapeHtml()/escHtml() that does not escape double quotes. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates inline handler injection. In most files used in element content (safe). In attribute contexts (title/aria-label/value/data-col) should add escapeAttr().

statistics.js esc() function (lines 448-452) does not escape double quotes — same pattern as escapeHtml() in other modules. Used in title attributes with PDF-derived font names (line 299) and image names (line 321). CSP mitigates. MEDIUM-LOW risk.

layers.js lines 505-507: OCG usage dictionary values (printState/viewState/exportState) from PDF inserted into innerHTML without escaping. A crafted PDF could inject HTML in the layer details popover. CSP blocks script execution. MEDIUM-LOW risk.
layers.js line 295,297,300: escapeHtml(group.name) in aria-label and title attributes — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Layer names from PDF OCG structure. CSP mitigates. LOW risk.
layers.js local downloadBlob() does not use sanitizeFilename from utils.js — download attribute prevents path traversal but inconsistent with other modules. LOW risk.
layers.js exportFlattenLayers: no processing timeout — renders all pages at 2x scale. Client-side self-DoS only for large PDFs. LOW risk.

bookmark-editor.js line 461: escapeHtml(item.title) in value attribute inside innerHTML template — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Bookmark titles from PDF outline or user input. CSP blocks inline handlers. Self-XSS only. LOW risk.
reading-progress.js line 387: bookmark name (bm.name) used unescaped in title attribute inside innerHTML. Self-XSS only — user sets own bookmark names, data in same-origin localStorage. LOW risk.
reading-progress.js line 550: escapeHtml(doc.name) in title attribute — escapeHtml doesn't escape double quotes. Self-XSS only — doc.name from user-selected file. LOW risk.

hyperlinks.js: detected PDF link URLs not validated for protocol scheme. A malicious PDF could contain javascript: URIs in link annotations. CSP + noopener/noreferrer mitigate. Should add protocol allowlist (http/https/mailto) before window.open().
hyperlinks.js line 686: email link window.open() missing noopener/noreferrer — minor since mailto: links are low risk.

context-menu.js line 649: window.open(ctx.url, '_blank', 'noopener') — opens PDF-derived URL without protocol allowlist. Same pattern as hyperlinks.js. A javascript: URI could execute if CSP does not block it. CSP script-src 'self' mitigates. MEDIUM-LOW risk.
img2pdf.js image validation uses OR logic (MIME type OR extension) - slightly permissive but acceptable for client-side processing.
insert-pages.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
metadata.js has dead code: escapeHtml() function defined but never called.
batch.js line 294: escapeHtml() used with textContent causes double-escaping — cosmetic display bug for filenames containing &, <, >.
keyboard-shortcuts.js line 416: document.write used on new popup window for print cheat sheet — acceptable pattern but noted for tracking.
headers-footers.js: custom preset names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, only affects local storage size.
form-data.js: template names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, same pattern as headers-footers.js.
page-labels.js: custom preset names from prompt() not length-limited before localStorage storage — minor, same pattern as headers-footers.js and form-data.js.

Session restore (TASK-036): restoreNotes/restoreAnnotations/restoreDrawings/restoreSignatures/restoreFormValues do not validate data schema from IndexedDB. IndexedDB is same-origin so risk is self-XSS only. Low priority.
link-manager.js Find & Replace: new RegExp() from user input could cause ReDoS with pathological patterns. Low impact — only runs against URL strings (typically short). Errors caught.
compare.js escapeHtml() does not escape double quotes — same pattern as other modules. Only used in element content context in HTML report, not in attributes. Safe.

snip.js line 865: innerHTML with template literal uses internally-generated values (pageNum, format, timestamp) — not user-controlled. LOW risk but should ideally use textContent/DOM API for defense-in-depth.
snip.js: no escapeHtml utility defined — acceptable since no user-controlled strings are inserted via innerHTML, but would be good practice to add.
snip.js: selections array unbounded — no max limit. Low risk since user-driven interaction.
snip.js line 661: raw err.message from clipboard API shown in toast — minor info disclosure, clipboard errors are browser-generated.

page-tabs.js importTabs() reads JSON file without schema validation — imported tab objects concatenated directly into allTabs. Malformed data could cause rendering errors. Not a direct security issue but poor input validation.
svg-export.js: no processing timeout on multi-page export loop — client-side self-DoS for large PDFs at high scale. LOW risk.
magnifier.js: buildHiResBuffer renders single page at 3x scale with no timeout — LOW risk, single-page only.

annotation-exchange.js: imported link URLs from XFDF/FDF not protocol-validated — same pre-existing pattern as hyperlinks.js. Malicious XFDF could contain javascript: URIs. CSP mitigates. MEDIUM-LOW risk.
